title: proof-construct grammar pinning (was jssm#818)
manager: Pin the grammar of the proof constructs — the exact, stable syntax that verified example machines are written in — so the proof-example corpus rests on a fixed target rather than a moving one. You can't build a durable corpus of proven machines on a grammar that shifts under it; pinning the constructs is the C3 slice that unblocks the corpus.
cs: C3 slice; blocks the proof-example corpus (#817)
